home.manifestoTitle: I help brands organize ideas and build visual languages with purpose.
home.aboutTeaser: The work starts by understanding context, questioning obvious paths, and turning concepts into consistent and long-lasting visual systems.
home.descriptionLeft: I work with visual identity and branding, helping brands structure ideas and build their own language. Every project starts from context and real needs—not ready-made solutions. I create and extend identity systems, art direction, editorial and presentation design, institutional materials, digital assets,
home.descriptionRight: and motion design—always thinking in systems, not isolated pieces. The process begins with conversation. Listening, exchanging, and testing paths is part of the work, especially when ideas are still forming. I favor decisions that sustain the concept over time, rather than quick answers or immediate effects.

about.title: ABOUT ME
about.paragraph1: My name is Arthur and I work with visual identity and branding, helping brands organize ideas and build their own language. I’m not interested in repeating ready-made solutions—every project starts by understanding context and questioning what’s already given. What needs to be said? And what can be said differently?
about.paragraph2: My practice involves creating and unfolding visual identities, art direction, editorial and presentation design, institutional materials, digital pieces, and motion design. But the work doesn’t start from a list—it starts by listening, understanding needs, and giving ideas space to grow. Questioning obvious paths is part of the process, as well as making visual choices that support the concept over time—not just immediate impact. More than arriving fast at an answer, the work is about asking the right questions and finding the best solutions.
about.paragraph3: This way of working is built between education and practice. I hold a degree in Advertising and a Master’s in Communication and Territorialities (UFES), and I’ve worked as an independent designer for over six years, with experience in projects for companies and cultural initiatives.
about.cta: So—how can I help you today?
